questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate &#39;one word &#39; for the expressions given below</p>

<p>One who is not sure about God&#39;s existence&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. </strong></p>

<p>To confirm with the help of evidence</p>

<p>Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words.</p>

<p>Several persons are now trained in <u>the use of fingers and hands to communicate and convey ideas</u> so that they can translate news for the differently abled persons.</p>

<p><strong>In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the alternative which is&nbsp;the best substitute of the phrase.</strong></p>

<p>A funeral song or tune</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Select the most appropriate word for the given group of words.</strong></p>

<p>A calculated move to gain an advantage&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.&nbsp;</strong></p>

<p>One who treats and prevents diseases of the heart&nbsp;</p>

<p>Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.</p>

<p>Showing a lack of experience, wisdom or judgment</p>

<p>Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.</p>

<p>One who specialises in the treatment of babies and children.</p>

<p>Choose the option that substitutes the given phrase appropriately.</p>

<p>Long and complicated series of events</p>

<p><strong>Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.</strong></p>

<p>A person giving too much attention to small details and wanting perfection.</p>

<p>Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.</p>

<p>Someone who is friendly and good natured</p>

<p><strong>Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.</strong></p>

<p>One who examines accounts officially</p>

<p>Substitute one word for the italicised expression.</p>

<p>As a port it was notorious for its <em><u>smuggling and the trade was prohibited by law</u></em>.&nbsp;</p>
&#39;Concord&#39; means
options: the agreement of the verb with the subject /
giving special importance to one part of a word or a sentence /
the style used in ordinary conversation /
a group of words with a special meaning /
